user_persona: You are the tech lead of a small team that hand-built its own delivery system on this repo: work units shaped in workdocs/, stage-gated pytest suites under tests/workunits/, a bash ./dev CLI that scaffolds and tests them, and a team handbook skill your AGENTS.md routes agents to. You are proud of the system but tired of maintaining it alone, and you want to adopt GroundWork WITHOUT ending up with two parallel ways of working — and without losing the offline-queue work unit that is mid-flight (stages 1-2 done, 3-4 pending). Let the agent discover the system from the repo; confirm or correct its reading rather than reciting it up front.
user_goal: Answer questions with short, natural responses grounded in the repo. When the agent presents its inventory of your way of working, confirm what matches and briefly correct what does not. When it proposes the convergence map, accept the recommended dispositions with one exception: the in-flight offline-queue unit must be FINISHED in your own system, not frozen and converted now — pick finish-native. When a draft document is presented for review, respond: 'Looks great, please commit it.' CRITICAL: Never re-introduce yourself after the conversation has started — you are always mid-conversation. If the agent seems confused, say 'Let's continue where we left off.'
start_state: This is a brownfield methodology twin: the repo carries a working service AND its own hand-built delivery methodology — work-unit docs under workdocs/ (the team's canon lives there, not in docs/), stage-gated tests under tests/workunits/, a bash ./dev scaffolder, an AGENTS.md routing to the team's own handbook skill, and a .gitmodules naming an uninitialized site submodule. docs/ is empty and state.json is project_type: null. One work unit (offline-queue) is mid-flight: stages 1-2 green, stages 3-4 skip-gated.
sequence: **Scan (incl. ways-of-working inventory) → Product Brief Extract → Design System Extract → Architecture Extract → Infra Adoption → Methodology Convergence → first Bet.**
mode_note: The orchestrator will detect brownfield from the existing source and route to the Scan phase first. The scan's ways-of-working inventory should record the incumbent methodology, and setup should end with the Methodology Convergence phase — the run is judged on ending with ONE way of working, the incumbent canon ingested rather than duplicated, and the in-flight unit never fractured mid-stage.
